title: Quarto + VS Code + GitHub Pages 操作手册
text:
这份文档总结了我们今天从零搭建一个 Quarto 网站，并把它发布到 GitHub Pages 的完整流程。内容尽量写得细一点，让没有做过的同学也能一步一步照着完成。
适用场景：

老师要求用 Quarto 做一个简单网站
用 VS Code 编辑
用 GitHub Pages 发布
最后把网站链接发给老师

最终目标：

本地能预览网站
GitHub 上有 repo
GitHub Pages 成功发布
拿到一个公开 URL




开始前先确认电脑上有：

VS Code
Git
Quarto
GitHub 账号

可以在终端里检查：
git --version
quarto --version
如果 quarto --version 能显示版本号，就说明 Quarto 已经装好了。




先新建一个文件夹，比如放在桌面：
mkdir ~/Desktop/my-website
cd ~/Desktop/my-website
然后用 VS Code 打开这个文件夹。




在 VS Code 的终端里运行：
quarto create project website .
意思是：在当前目录创建一个 Quarto 网站项目。
执行完成后，一般会看到这些文件：

_quarto.yml
index.qmd
about.qmd
styles.css

说明框架已经搭好了。






这是网站配置文件，用来控制：

网站类型
网站标题
顶部导航栏
主题
CSS
输出目录




这是首页内容。



这是 About 页面内容。



这是自定义样式文件，如果想改颜色、字体、间距，可以在这里加 CSS。





这一步非常重要，因为 GitHub Pages 可以直接从 repo 里的 docs/ 文件夹发布网站。
打开 _quarto.yml，改成下面这种结构：
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs

website:
  title: "hello"
  navbar:
    left:
      - href: index.qmd
        text: Home
      - href: about.qmd
        text: About

format:
  html:
    theme:
      - cosmo
      - brand
    css: styles.css
    toc: true
这里最关键的是这一行：
output-dir: docs
它表示 Quarto 渲染后的网站文件会输出到 docs/ 文件夹。




在终端里运行：
quarto preview
运行后终端会出现一个本地地址，例如：
http://localhost:3209/
或者：
http://localhost:4473/
把这个地址复制到浏览器里，就能看到你的网站。
这一步的作用是：

检查网站能不能正常显示
看看首页和 About 页面是否正确
修改内容后能实时预览

如果想停止本地预览，在终端里按：
Ctrl + C




当你确认页面没问题后，运行：
quarto render
这一步会把 .qmd 文件变成真正的网站文件，并输出到 docs/。
执行完之后，你的项目目录里应该会出现：

docs/index.html
docs/about.html
docs/site_libs/...

如果有 docs/ 文件夹，说明这一步成功了。




去 GitHub 新建一个 repository。
建议设置：

Repository name: 自己起一个名字，比如 Fintech-533
Visibility: Public

建 repo 时建议不要勾选：

Add a README file
.gitignore
license

因为你本地已经有项目文件了，建一个空 repo 最方便。




假设你的 GitHub repo 地址是：
https://github.com/jxin00721/Fintech-533.git
那么在本地项目目录终端里依次运行：
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial Quarto website"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin https://github.com/jxin00721/Fintech-533.git
git push -u origin main
如果之前已经初始化过 git，就不用重复 git init。
如果 remote 已经存在，也不要重复 git remote add origin ...。
常见更新流程会变成：
git add .
git commit -m "Update site"
git push




push 成功之后，去 GitHub repo 页面，按下面步骤设置：

打开你的 repo
点击 Settings
在左侧找到 Pages
在 Build and deployment 下设置：


Source: Deploy from a branch
Branch: main
Folder: /docs


点击 Save

这一步非常关键，因为老师的要求就是让 GitHub Pages 从你放网站文件的目录发布。我们这里用的就是 docs/。




保存后等待 1 到 3 分钟，然后刷新 GitHub Pages 页面。
如果成功，你会看到类似：
Your site is live at https://你的用户名.github.io/仓库名/
比如我们实际成功的例子是：
https://jxin00721.github.io/Fintech-533/
这说明：

网站已经部署成功
GitHub Pages 设置正确
现在可以公开访问





老师最后还要求：

add a picture
push to Git
message me

所以还要补一张图片。


在项目目录里新建一个文件夹：
images/
然后把一张图片放进去，例如：
images/me.jpg



打开 index.qmd，加入：
![](images/me.jpg){width=40%}
一个更完整的首页示例可以写成：
---
title: "Fintech533 Pairs Trading"
---

Welcome to my Quarto website for Fintech 533.

![](images/me.jpg){width=40%}

This site is for practicing GitHub Pages and Quarto.



quarto render
git add .
git commit -m "Add image to site"
git push
然后等一两分钟，刷新网站页面，就能看到图片了。





老师最后要的是你的网站链接。
可以直接发：
https://你的用户名.github.io/仓库名/
也可以礼貌一点发成：
Hi Professor, here is my site URL:
https://你的用户名.github.io/仓库名/




以后如果你改了 index.qmd、about.qmd、图片或者 CSS，更新流程就是固定这几步：
quarto render
git add .
git commit -m "Update site"
git push
然后等待 GitHub Pages 自动重新部署。






原因：

repo 里还没有内容
或者还没有 docs/

解决：
先本地运行：
quarto render
再把生成的 docs/ push 到 GitHub。



可能原因：

GitHub Pages 还在部署
docs/ 里没有 index.html
Pages 设置不是 main + /docs

解决：

等 1 到 3 分钟再刷新
检查 docs/index.html 是否存在
检查 Settings > Pages 的设置




原因：

只运行了 quarto preview
没有运行 quarto render
没有 git push

解决：
quarto render
git add .
git commit -m "Update site"
git push



最好在 _quarto.yml 里写完整格式：
navbar:
  left:
    - href: index.qmd
      text: Home
    - href: about.qmd
      text: About
不要只写一行：
- about.qmd
虽然有时也能工作，但不够清晰。





如果以后要快速重复这件事，可以直接记这套：
mkdir my-website
cd my-website
quarto create project website .
修改 _quarto.yml：
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs
然后：
quarto preview
quarto render
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial site"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin 你的repo地址
git push -u origin main
最后去 GitHub：

Settings
Pages
Source = Deploy from a branch
Branch = main
Folder = /docs

完成后就能拿到公开网站链接。




这次实际完成的闭环是：

用 Quarto 在本地创建了网站项目
在 VS Code 中编辑 _quarto.yml、index.qmd、about.qmd
把输出目录改成了 docs
用 quarto preview 在本地预览
用 quarto render 生成网站
把项目 push 到 GitHub repo
在 GitHub Pages 里选择 main 分支的 /docs
成功得到公开网址
按老师要求，下一步只需要补图片并再次 push





这套流程的核心其实很简单：

用 Quarto 写网站内容，用 VS Code 编辑，用 quarto render 生成 docs/，再让 GitHub Pages 从 main/docs 发布。

title: Breakout Strategy Backtest
text:
This page presents a complete breakout trading assignment built around a long-only Donchian-style breakout rule, a rolling walk-forward backtest, a downloadable trade blotter, a daily ledger, and out-of-sample performance analysis.


The strategy buys an asset when its closing price finishes above the highest high from the prior 20 trading days, which is intended to capture the start of a bullish trend. Trades are entered at the next day’s open, risk is controlled with a stop-loss placed 2.0 ATR below the entry price, profits are taken at 2.0 ATR above the entry price, and any position that remains open for 15 trading days is closed at the next open. The backtest uses one year of training followed by one quarter of out-of-sample testing, then rolls that process forward across the sample.



The core detection function is named detect_breakouts. It computes a shifted rolling high and flags a breakout only when the current close is above the highest high from the previous lookback window, which avoids look-ahead bias.
def detect_breakouts(data: pd.DataFrame, breakout_lookback: int) -> pd.DataFrame:
    breakout_frame = data.copy()
    breakout_frame["rolling_high"] = (
        breakout_frame["high"].rolling(window=breakout_lookback).max().shift(1)
    )
    breakout_frame["breakout_signal"] = (
        breakout_frame["close"] > breakout_frame["rolling_high"]
    ) & breakout_frame["rolling_high"].notna()
    return breakout_frame
All thresholds are explicitly defined in the analysis code:

BREAKOUT_LOOKBACK = 20 after walk-forward selection from {10, 20, 55}
ATR_WINDOW = 14
STOP_LOSS_ATR_MULTIPLE = 2.0
PROFIT_TARGET_ATR_MULTIPLE = 2.0
MAX_HOLD_DAYS = 15
RISK_FREE_RATE = 0.02




I screened the ETF universe SPY, QQQ, IWM, XLE, GLD, and TLT using a rolling walk-forward framework with 252 trading days of training and 63 trading days of out-of-sample testing. XLE was selected because it delivered the strongest out-of-sample Sharpe ratio in the screen. The top three assets by walk-forward Sharpe were XLE, QQQ, and IWM.
Data retrieval is implemented with a ShinyBroker-first loader. The code first tries to request daily history through shinybroker.fetch_historical_data(...) so it can use a local IBKR/TWS session when one is available. In this run, there was no active local TWS connection, so the loader automatically fell back to a public Yahoo chart endpoint. That fallback is documented in the notebook and code so the workflow remains reproducible.

The breakout rule is:
Close_t > max(High_{t-20}, ..., High_{t-1})
In plain English, a breakout occurs when today’s closing price is above the highest daily high from the previous 20 sessions. The rolling high is shifted by one day so the strategy only uses information that would have been known at the close. ATR uses a 14-day window. The stop-loss is entry price - 2.0 * ATR, the profit target is entry price + 2.0 * ATR, and the timeout rule closes any open trade after 15 trading days.



The metrics below are computed on the stitched out-of-sample ledger. The annualized Sharpe ratio assumes a 2.0% annual risk-free rate.



<table class="dataframe table table-striped table-sm">

0.669


Gross profits divided by gross losses.




Expectancy (USD)


$-594.87


Average dollar PnL per trade.




Number of trades


16


Completed out-of-sample trades in the stitched walk-forward test.



























Every completed trade is classified into one of three mutually exclusive outcomes:

Successful: the close reached the profit target and the strategy exited at the next open
Timed out: the trade remained open for 15 trading days and was closed with a market order at the next open
Stop-loss triggered: the close fell to or below the stop-loss threshold and the strategy exited at the next open






The full trade blotter and daily ledger are available as downloadable CSV files:

Download breakout blotter CSV
Download breakout ledger CSV
Download walk-forward window summary CSV
Download asset screening results CSV

Readable versions are embedded below.

Data frequency: daily OHLCV bars
Strategy direction: long only
Position sizing: fixed notional of $100,000 per trade, rounded down to whole shares
Slippage and commissions: not included
Risk-free rate assumption: 2.0% annually
Walk-forward design: 252-day training window and 63-day out-of-sample test window

The underlying analysis files are also available:

Download the Python analysis script
Download the Jupyter notebook




The final out-of-sample result is mixed rather than spectacular, which is useful in its own right. XLE produced the best walk-forward Sharpe in the ETF screen, but the strategy still had a modest average trade return and a non-trivial drawdown. That is a realistic outcome for a simple breakout rule: the framework is transparent, easy to audit, and capable of catching trends, but it is still sensitive to whipsaws and the exact exit logic. For a future extension, I would test volume confirmation, volatility filters, and a wider asset universe.

title: Breakout Strategy Backtest
text: The strategy buys an asset when its closing price finishes above the highest high from the prior 20 trading days, which is intended to capture the start of a bullish trend. Trades are entered at the next day’s open, risk is controlled with a stop-loss placed 2.0 ATR below the entry price, profits are taken at 2.0 ATR above the entry price, and any position that remains open for 15 trading days is closed at the next open. The backtest uses one year of training followed by one quarter of out-of-sample testing, then rolls that process forward across the sample.

title: Breakout Strategy Backtest
text:
The core detection function is named detect_breakouts. It computes a shifted rolling high and flags a breakout only when the current close is above the highest high from the previous lookback window, which avoids look-ahead bias.
def detect_breakouts(data: pd.DataFrame, breakout_lookback: int) -> pd.DataFrame:
    breakout_frame = data.copy()
    breakout_frame["rolling_high"] = (
        breakout_frame["high"].rolling(window=breakout_lookback).max().shift(1)
    )
    breakout_frame["breakout_signal"] = (
        breakout_frame["close"] > breakout_frame["rolling_high"]
    ) & breakout_frame["rolling_high"].notna()
    return breakout_frame
All thresholds are explicitly defined in the analysis code:

BREAKOUT_LOOKBACK = 20 after walk-forward selection from {10, 20, 55}
ATR_WINDOW = 14
STOP_LOSS_ATR_MULTIPLE = 2.0
PROFIT_TARGET_ATR_MULTIPLE = 2.0
MAX_HOLD_DAYS = 15
RISK_FREE_RATE = 0.02

title: Breakout Strategy Backtest
text:
I screened the ETF universe SPY, QQQ, IWM, XLE, GLD, and TLT using a rolling walk-forward framework with 252 trading days of training and 63 trading days of out-of-sample testing. XLE was selected because it delivered the strongest out-of-sample Sharpe ratio in the screen. The top three assets by walk-forward Sharpe were XLE, QQQ, and IWM.
Data retrieval is implemented with a ShinyBroker-first loader. The code first tries to request daily history through shinybroker.fetch_historical_data(...) so it can use a local IBKR/TWS session when one is available. In this run, there was no active local TWS connection, so the loader automatically fell back to a public Yahoo chart endpoint. That fallback is documented in the notebook and code so the workflow remains reproducible.

title: Breakout Strategy Backtest
text:
The breakout rule is:
Close_t > max(High_{t-20}, ..., High_{t-1})
In plain English, a breakout occurs when today’s closing price is above the highest daily high from the previous 20 sessions. The rolling high is shifted by one day so the strategy only uses information that would have been known at the close. ATR uses a 14-day window. The stop-loss is entry price - 2.0 * ATR, the profit target is entry price + 2.0 * ATR, and the timeout rule closes any open trade after 15 trading days.

title: Breakout Strategy Backtest
text:
The metrics below are computed on the stitched out-of-sample ledger. The annualized Sharpe ratio assumes a 2.0% annual risk-free rate.

title: Breakout Strategy Backtest
text:
Every completed trade is classified into one of three mutually exclusive outcomes:

Successful: the close reached the profit target and the strategy exited at the next open
Timed out: the trade remained open for 15 trading days and was closed with a market order at the next open
Stop-loss triggered: the close fell to or below the stop-loss threshold and the strategy exited at the next open

title: Breakout Strategy Backtest
Breakout Strategy Backtest
text:
Data frequency: daily OHLCV bars
Strategy direction: long only
Position sizing: fixed notional of $100,000 per trade, rounded down to whole shares
Slippage and commissions: not included
Risk-free rate assumption: 2.0% annually
Walk-forward design: 252-day training window and 63-day out-of-sample test window

text: The final out-of-sample result is mixed rather than spectacular, which is useful in its own right. XLE produced the best walk-forward Sharpe in the ETF screen, but the strategy still had a modest average trade return and a non-trivial drawdown. That is a realistic outcome for a simple breakout rule: the framework is transparent, easy to audit, and capable of catching trends, but it is still sensitive to whipsaws and the exact exit logic. For a future extension, I would test volume confirmation, volatility filters, and a wider asset universe.

title: Methodology and Files
text:
This project was built in two workspaces:

Analysis and backtest code: /Users/jinxin/Desktop/Fintech 533
Public website: /Users/jinxin/Desktop/my-website



Main analysis outputs:

Breakout_Strategy_Assignment.ipynb
breakout_strategy_assignment.py
breakout_blotter.csv
breakout_ledger.csv
breakout_walkforward_windows.csv

Website artifacts:

artifacts/data/*
artifacts/tables/*
artifacts/plots/*





Run the analysis script or notebook in the Fintech 533 folder.
The pipeline tries shinybroker first for historical data and falls back to a public Yahoo chart endpoint if no local IBKR/TWS session is available.
The walk-forward backtest exports CSVs, HTML tables, and HTML charts directly into the website’s artifacts folder.
Run quarto render inside my-website.
Push the updated docs/ folder to GitHub Pages.





The breakout rule is intentionally simple so the methodology stays transparent.
The asset screen is based on ETFs because they provide liquid, easy-to-explain daily breakout candidates.
The page links on the home screen are the easiest way to download the final blotter, ledger, notebook, and script.
